description: tu93 1/9. L1 solved via BFS path (18 moves). L2 BLOCKED: arrow entity at (27,18) rot=270 kills player when approaching from LEFT via corridor. Solver BFS ignores entity interactions. Arrow activates when player at x=21 (6px from arrow), moves LEFT toward player = death. Need alternate approach: either find hidden path or understand arrow mechanic differently. Each env.step() = one complete player turn (8 frames for movement + entity settling).

tu93 step mechanics: each env.step(direction) is ONE frame in a 3-phase cycle. Phase 0: player input. Phase 1: player settling + arrow activation. Phase 2: entity settling. SDK returns 8 frames for a complete turn. 1 frame = blocked move. 15 frames = death/game_over. Grid nodes at maze-relative positions divisible by 6. Player sprite has yellow indicator pixel at [0,1] that shifts with rotation.
L1 VERIFIED: 18 moves. RIGHT DOWN DOWN RIGHT UP RIGHT DOWN DOWN LEFT LEFT DOWN RIGHT RIGHT DOWN RIGHT UP RIGHT DOWN. Simple maze pathfinding, no entities.

tu93 3/9. CORE MECHANIC: arrows are LETHAL — activate from specific direction (6px away, arrow faces TOWARD player), then arrow moves to player and kills in 3 shrink cycles. SOLUTION: destroy arrows by walking INTO them from a NON-ACTIVATION direction. Player destroys entities on contact (pbknvgksfm). BFS with arrow-alive state tracking finds safe paths. L4 introduces BOUNCERS — moving entities that reverse at walls. Need bouncer position simulation.

ARROW ACTIVATION POSITIONS (CORRECTED): rot=0(UP)->player at (ax,ay-6) ABOVE. rot=180(DOWN)->player at (ax,ay+6) BELOW. rot=90(RIGHT)->player at (ax+6,ay) RIGHT. rot=270(LEFT)->player at (ax-6,ay) LEFT. Arrow faces TOWARD the player position. Walking into arrow position from ANY OTHER direction destroys the arrow safely.
L2 VERIFIED: 10 moves. UP RIGHT RIGHT DOWN RIGHT RIGHT UP RIGHT RIGHT UP. Bypass arrow at (27,18) via lower corridor. Arrow faces LEFT, danger at (33,18). Take path through y=24 row to get past.

L3 VERIFIED: 19 moves. Must destroy ALL 3 arrows: (12,6)rot90 from (12,0)DOWN, (18,6)rot180 from (24,6)LEFT after arrow1 dead, (0,18)rot90 from (0,12)DOWN. Path: UP UP RIGHT UP LEFT LEFT UP LEFT LEFT DOWN RIGHT DOWN LEFT LEFT LEFT DOWN RIGHT DOWN RIGHT.

L4 BLOCKED by bouncer at (18,6) oscillating in x=18 column. Player must traverse (18,24)→(18,0) = 4 UP steps while bouncer oscillates same column with period ~8. No timing window for 4 consecutive safe UPs. Player CAN shrink bouncer by walking into it (pbknvgksfm→zftruguysu→ojcejjkbwt) but bouncer takes 3 hits to destroy and moves after each hit. Needs interactive play or deeper entity simulation.

tu93 4/9. L4 SOLVED with precise bouncer timing: waste steps at (24,18)→(24,12) to offset bouncer period. Key: bouncer simulation needs 1-step offset from initial position (bouncers move BEFORE first player action). L5 has 4 bouncers with incommensurate periods — no simple waste-step timing found. Needs full game state simulation or perceptual approach.

L4 VERIFIED: 17 moves. RIGHT RIGHT LEFT RIGHT RIGHT RIGHT UP UP DOWN UP LEFT UP UP LEFT LEFT DOWN LEFT. Bouncer timing: 3 waste at start (RIGHT LEFT RIGHT = timing offset), then go via (24,24)→(24,12) with DOWN UP waste to cross (18,12) when bouncer at (18,24).

L5 UNSOLVED. 4 bouncers with different periods: horizontal (0,18)rot90 sweeps x-axis, vertical (18,12)rot180 and (18,24)rot0 sweep y-axis, vertical (12,18)rot180 sweeps short range. 4 simple paths to exit, none solvable with waste timing at junctions. Bouncer periods are incommensurate — no timing window exists for simple paths. Needs: (a) path through extra nodes to create offset, (b) full SDK-verified BFS, or (c) interactive visual play.

L5 VERIFIED: 29 moves via extended eastern approach. Go LEFT to (0,0), south to (6,30), east to (24,30), UP to (24,18), LEFT to exit. Key: approach exit from EAST side to avoid central bouncers. 3 waste pairs at start for timing. Path: LEFT RIGHT LEFT RIGHT LEFT RIGHT LEFT*7 DOWN*3 RIGHT DOWN*2 RIGHT*3 UP DOWN UP DOWN UP*2 LEFT.
tu93 5/9. L5 solved with eastern approach (29 moves). L6 has 6 arrows + 2 bouncers, period-2 bouncer blocks all direct paths. Need arrow destruction or full game-state simulation.

BOUNCER TIMING CRITICAL: bouncers are NOT player-independent. Walking into a bouncer position changes its trajectory. Blocked moves do NOT advance entity states. Bouncer timing has even/odd parity that cannot be shifted with blocked-move waste. Must find paths with correct natural parity or destroy blocking entities first.

L6: 236 simple paths tested via SDK, none survive. The level requires NODE REVISITS (waste steps for bouncer timing or arrow destruction + backtrack). Simple paths can't solve it. The bouncer at (18,6) with period-2 blocks all direct routes. The only path around (via y=18→y=24) crosses arrow danger zones at (12,24). Destroying arrow at (12,18) allows reaching (12,24) but arrow at (12,30) then kills. Multi-arrow destruction chain needed: (12,36)→UP(12,30)→UP(12,24)→LEFT(6,24)→UP*3→EXIT. Requires reaching (12,36) first — which goes through more arrow territories. THIS LEVEL NEEDS INTERACTIVE PERCEPTUAL PLAY.

L6 DEEP ANALYSIS: 6 arrows form interlocking dependency chain. Destroying one exposes player to another's activation zone. Correct destruction order: (30,18)→(18,36)→(12,36)→(18,30)→(12,30)→(12,18). But 2 bouncers patrol paths between destruction targets. Bouncer at (18,6) has period-2 with locked parity (unreachable on even steps from start). Bouncer at (36,30) sweeps bottom row. Arrow-bouncer interaction makes timing extremely complex. NEED PERCEPTUAL APPROACH — play interactively watching bouncer positions frame by frame.

tu93 5/9 FINAL for this session. L6 is a multi-bouncer multi-arrow timing puzzle that resists analytical solving. 2000+ paths tested via SDK oracle. Arrow destruction chain identified but bouncer timing makes every variant fail. BOUNCERS ARE INVINCIBLE — cannot be destroyed by player contact (kills player instead). L6 solution exists but requires either full game-state BFS (too slow at 3.5 tests/sec) or interactive perceptual play with human coaching. Moving to a different game.

L6 VERIFIED: 32 moves. PERCEPTUAL APPROACH WORKED where analytical failed. Arrow destruction chain: (30,18)→(18,36)→(12,36)→(18,30)→(12,30)→(12,18). Route through y=36 avoids bottom bouncer. Key: reactive bouncer avoidance (check position after each step, insert waste when needed) instead of pre-computing timing. Path: LEFT LEFT DOWN DOWN RIGHT DOWN DOWN LEFT DOWN LEFT LEFT RIGHT RIGHT LEFT RIGHT UP LEFT LEFT RIGHT RIGHT RIGHT UP UP LEFT LEFT LEFT DOWN LEFT UP UP UP LEFT.
L7 VERIFIED: 14 moves. No bouncers, 1 arrow + 1 delayed entity. Arrow destruction + simple pathfinding. RIGHT RIGHT RIGHT DOWN DOWN RIGHT UP RIGHT UP UP UP RIGHT DOWN DOWN.

tu93 7/9. L6 SOLVED via perceptual approach (32 moves, arrow destruction chain + reactive bouncer avoidance). L7 solved (14 moves). L8 has delayed entity that follows player directions with lag — falls back to own facing when replayed direction blocked. Creates unavoidable collision in x=24 corridor. Need new approach.

L8 BLOCKED by delayed entity at (24,6). Delayed entity REVERSES player directions (RIGHT→LEFT, UP→DOWN). Queue starts with [own_rot, own_rot] (2 DOWNs) then reversed player dirs. Entity chases player through the only path to exit (x=24 column). Activation range 12px, activates from specific direction like arrows. 200+ paths tested. Entity always catches player at (24,12) or follows into the corridor. Need deeper source analysis of delayed entity queue processing or interactive coaching session.

L8 DELAYED ENTITY MECHANICS (verified from source): entity queue starts [180,180] (own facing x2), then player's ACTUAL directions appended. Entity processes one queued direction per turn. Blocked moves (player hitting wall) do NOT append to queue or advance entities. Entity replays player's EXACT directions (not reversed). Entity is 2 turns behind due to initial [180,180]. From (24,6) entity goes DOWN DOWN to (24,18) in 2 turns, then follows player. Only valid moves from (24,18): UP (toward entity), LEFT, RIGHT. Going LEFT feeds entity LEFT direction = entity goes LEFT from (24,18) = AWAY from corridor. But entity arrives at (24,18) on step 8 regardless. Player cannot return to (24,18) at step 8. Need 3+ step detour through L/R before sprinting UP. All paths through x=24 tested — entity always intercepts due to 2-turn lag and corridor structure.

L8 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: blocked moves freeze ALL entity state (no Phase 2 runs). Verified: 3 blocked DOWNs at (24,18) keep entity at (24,6). But first VALID UP still collides — entity settles at (24,12) in Phase 2 of the same turn the player arrives there. Entity processes queue [180,180,player_dirs...]. Two simple paths exist to exit, BOTH go through (24,18) triggering entity. 1000+ L/R waste combos tested. Entity is invincible and follows player directions with 2-turn lag. NEED INTERACTIVE COACHING to find the missing mechanic.

L8 Rule 0 applied: played visually, watched entity move step by step. Confirmed: entity moves DOWN from (24,6) one grid node per valid player move. Blocked moves freeze ALL state. Tried RIGHT from (24,18) to (30,18), then blocked UPs (froze entity), then LEFT — entity catches up in Phase 2. Tried going RIGHT past entity column — (30,18) has NO UP path. Every approach converges to same collision: player and entity meet at (24,12) or (24,18) during Phase 2 settling. Only 2 simple paths to exit exist, BOTH through (24,18). GENUINE KNOWLEDGE GAP — need coaching or fresh perspective.

L8 SOLVED: 21 moves. KEY INSIGHT: lead the delayed entity on a goose chase through the southern maze loop. Entity follows player directions with 2-turn lag. Go LEFT LEFT LEFT DOWN DOWN RIGHT UP UP (entity follows into south). Then sprint RIGHT RIGHT UP UP UP LEFT LEFT to exit while entity is deep in the loop. Path: RIGHT RIGHT UP UP RIGHT RIGHT LEFT LEFT LEFT DOWN DOWN RIGHT UP UP RIGHT RIGHT UP UP UP LEFT LEFT.

tu93 8/9! L8 SOLVED with goose chase (21 moves). L9 in progress: entity activates on first LEFT move, bouncer at (18,6) has period 6, entity and bouncer timing must be coordinated. Need to pass through (18,6) when bouncer is away AND entity is elsewhere. Multiple approaches tested, each collision teaches more about the timing. Close to solution.

L8 VERIFIED: 21 moves. GOOSE CHASE strategy: lead delayed entity through southern loop while sprinting to exit. Path: RIGHT RIGHT UP UP RIGHT RIGHT LEFT LEFT LEFT DOWN DOWN RIGHT UP UP RIGHT RIGHT UP UP UP LEFT LEFT.

content: L9 SOLVED (29 steps). Most complex level: 2 arrows + 3 bouncers + 1 delayed entity. Player at grid(4,4), exit at grid(4,5). Arrow1 at (3,5) faces RIGHT with activation zone = exit (4,5). Arrow2 at (3,6) faces UP with activation at (3,5). Delayed entity at (3,2) activates when player reaches (3,4) — the ONLY first move. Bouncer at (3,1) has period 6 matching minimum path length. Solution: BFS on simulator built from game source code. Key insight: waste steps (RIGHT DOWN DOWN LEFT) at turns 5-8 create a detour through (3,3)→(3,4)→(2,4)→(2,3) that shifts bouncer timing by 6 turns. Entity follows with 2-turn lag but waste directions get blocked when entity replays them. Player takes 3 entity/bouncer hits during eastern traverse but survives (3 shrink stages before removal). Phase model: Phase0=player, Phase1=player settle+bouncer 1px+entity 1px, Phase2=entity settle+overlap check+bouncer direction+entity activation. Critical: overlap check at entity DESTINATION not starting position — player can walk onto departing entity position safely.
